title: RNA Direct Lysis Method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6tsk7","text":"RNA Direct Lysis method","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1d6id","text":"(from Masse and Gottesman. 2003 Genes and Development. 17:2374-2383) (notes and italics are from E. Fozo by Selene Hess)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3g1ud","text":"Direct Lysis solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66r42","text":"320 mM NaAcetate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5klt3","text":"8% SDS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"26dka","text":"16 mM EDTA","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2q4o2","text":"N.B. Direct lysis solution will not stay in solution at RT. Prior to use, must get into solution by placing at 37 °C. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a74od","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"9cirr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"2isa1","text":"The recipe must be altered for use from minimal media.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
steps: At desired OD, remove 750 ml of cells to a tube at 65°C containing 500 ml acid:phenol-chloroform with 100 ml of direct lysis solution ( SET-UP at least 25 minutes prior to harvesting to ensure the components are at 65°C ). Vortex to mix, and place the tube back at 65°C for 10-15 minutes. Can store at -80 indefinitely. Once move on, can’t stop till step 7, ethanol.
Spin 13000 r.p.m., room temperature for 5-10 minutes. (15-20 minutes if from -80 storage)
Transfer supernatant to a new tube containing 500 ml of 65°C acid phenol: chloroform. Spin as in step 3 (RT) .
Transfer supernatant and extract with 400 ml “regular” phenol: chloroform. Gentle vortex . Spin as in step 3 (RT) . May need to do additional extractions at this point.

Transfer supernatant to a new tube and extract with 400 ml chloroform. Spin as in step 3 ( RT ). N.B. Liz and Aixia are the only ones who do this step, so it is clearly optional! In interface is thick, white goo.
Transfer supernatant and add 700 ml 99% ethanol ( 95% is fine ). Place in -80 C for 20 minutes ( or indefinitely ).
Spin 13000 r.p.m. 4°C , 30 minutes. Wash with 400uL 70% ethanol. Let pellet air dry.
Let sit for 10 minutes at RT and finish resuspending by pipetting up and down/flicking tube.
creator: Eadewunm
